name: Cefoperazone
description: Semisynthetic broad-spectrum cephalosporin with a tetrazolyl moiety that is resistant to beta-lactamase. It has been proposed especially against Pseudomonas infections.
indication: For the treatment of bacterial infections caused by susceptible microorganisms.
toxicity: Symptoms of overdose include blood in the urine, diarrhea, nausea, upper abdominal pain, and vomiting.
mechanismOfAction: Like all beta-lactam antibiotics, cefoperazone binds to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, causing the inhibition of the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins.
pharmacodynamics: Cefoperazone is a third generation cephalosporin antibiotic. Cefoperazone exerts its bactericidal effect by inhibiting the bacterial cell wall synthesis

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefoperazone.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefoperazone.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefoperazone may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefoperazone can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
